arts.ttl: Reading legend on 'horrendous' off-field crisis and 'remarkable' on-field success
arts.sum: Former Reading legend Steve Sidwell has labelled the work Noel Hunt has done 'remarkable' as they continue to hunt down the play-offs. Sidwell made nearly 200 appearances in four-and-a-half years with the club, winning two promotions before joining Chelsea and enjoying a Premier League career with the likes of Fulham and Stoke City. Now a prominent pundit, the former midfielder was on Sky Sports and discussed the ongoing off-field crisis at the club, labelling it 'horrendous' and 'never-ending.' However, in spite of this Hunt and his Royals sit just a point outside the play-offs with seven matches remaining. "Off the field, it’s nothing short of horrendous, said Sidwell. "Dai Yongge went in there- probably seeing a quick way to the Premier League- and threw a lot of money in at the start. He went about it in the wrong manner with ridiculous wages and then it snowballed. Off the pitch it’s very complex with a lot of parties involved and legal action. I think there’s not a lot of noise because people don’t know what to say in case they get in trouble.

arts.ttl: Preview: Reading vs. Wycombe Wanderers - prediction, team news, lineups
arts.sum: Sports Mole previews Saturday's League One clash between Reading and Wycombe Wanderers, including predictions, team news and possible lineups.

arts.ttl: Reading owner Yongge given extension to sell club
arts.sum: Reading owner Dai Yongge is given an extension to his deadline where he must divest his interests in the club.

arts.ttl: Reading given additional time to complete sale following EFL board meeting
arts.sum: The English Football League have given Reading a stay of execution at their latest meeting, giving Dai Yongge a further 19 days to sell his shares. The EFL disqualified Mr Yongge as an owner in February and gave him until today to show clear progress was being made in a sale or threatened to suspend the club from playing matches. In a statement released by the EFL this afternoon, the governing body have confirmed that "the Board recognises that these matters are further complicated by the structure of assets held across different companies, and various competing security interests" and have therefore extended the deadline to April 22. It goes on to say that they will monitor progress made with the sales pro

arts.ttl: Reading owner granted sale extension by EFL amid suspension threat
arts.sum: Dai Yongge given until 22 April to divest interests in club EFL believes Dai more motivated than previously to sell Reading have been granted an extension by the English Football League until 22 April for their owner, Dai Yongge, to sell the club. Reading had been at risk of suspension if they failed to show adequate signs of progress. Dai was disqualified under the league’s owners’ and directors’ test in February owing to debts and court rulings in his native China but the troubled League One club were permitted an extension on the initial 28-day period in which Dai needed to divest his shares, until this Saturday. The EFL, whose board met at their monthly meeting on Thursday, has now given Dai more time to sell. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Kick-off moved for traditional Inflatables Day as Reading travel to Bristol Rovers
arts.sum: Reading are set for another early start later this month as their clash at Bristol Rovers has been selected for television coverage. As the reverse fixture did back in September, the Royals' trip to the Memorial Stadium on April 26 will now kick-off at 12.30pm as opposed to the original time of 3pm. This is the final away match of the League One campaign and will sell out, as well as hold the traditional 'Inflatables Day' that has come with every final away day for the last decade. Last season, Reading won 2-0 in a match which all-but secured their third tier status, bringing joyous scenes on the terrace and on the field.

Reading picked the worst possible time to serve up one of their worst performances of the season against Blackpool after the 3-0 defeat saw them miss the chance to climb into the top six. The Royals ...
